chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 240 of 2018 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 15.10.2018 Syed Tajuddin, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, Petitioner Muhammad Asif has prayed for the grant of Succession Certificate in respect of moveable properties/assets, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Properties attached with the memo of petition, available at page 31 to 35, which were left by his deceased father, namely, Muhammad Siddique s/o. Muhammad Hashim, who died on 03.04.2018 at Karachi, leaving behind Muhammad Asif (petitioner) and Muhammad Junaid as his legal heirs. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA, bank statement, Defence Saving Certificates and bonus share are also filed at page 43 to 51. Legal heir Muhammad Junaid has filed his affidavit of no objection for the grant of Succession Certificate in favour of the petitioner. Two witnesses, namely, Muhammad Munaf s/o. Muhammad Amin and Muhammad Jawed s/o. Jaffar Khan have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. 2 Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 31.08.2018; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 27.09.2018. Today above named legal heir and witnesses are present and affirm the contents of their affidavits and also verify their signatures thereon.

Their affidavits are also available on record. 2 Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 31.08.2018; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 27.09.2018. Today above named legal heir and witnesses are present and affirm the contents of their affidavits and also verify their signatures thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Succession Certificate in respect of movable properties/assets shown in the Schedule of Properties, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
